COURT TRIAL CALENDAR ON SEP-21-2011 IN DEPT. 414 - TRIAL AS TO LIABILITY PROCEEDS, WITNESSES ARE AFFIRMED AND EXAMINED, BOTH SIDES REST. COUNSEL ARE TO FILE ADDITIONAL BRIEFS WITHIN 21 DAYS AT WHICH TIME THE MATTER WILL BE DEEMED SUBMITTED. THE COURT SHALL RENDER A DECISION 10 DAYS THEREAFTER. AFTER THE COURT RENDERS ITS DECISION, COUNSEL WILL ENTER INTO A STIPULATION TO BE PRESENTED TO THE PRESIDING JUDGE TO RESCHEDULE THE MATTER WITHIN 30 TO 60 DAYS FOR TRIAL AS TO THE DAMAGES PHASE. Ntc Of Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Of Settlement
Off Calendar Sep-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF MARTINA MUELLER'S MOTION FOR ORDER DETERMINING GOOD FAITH OF SETTLEMENT (WITH KLAUS KUBLER) is granted in part. The settlement amount and the payment plan is found to be in good faith under CCP section 877.6(a), notwithstanding the provision regarding allocation of damages. The court further finds that the allocation of damages in a settlement agreement or determined by a court at a C.C.P. section 877.6 hearing are not binding at the time of trial. Greathouse v. Amcord (1995) 35 Cal. App. 4th 831, 841; Gouvis Engineering v. Superior Court (1995) 37 Cal. App. 4th 642, 650-651. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
